title: euphrasia brownii var. paludosa
intro: Euphrasia brownii var. paludosa, also known as marsh eyebright, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Gentianaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its small, yellow or purple flowers and oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Euphrasia brownii var. paludosa can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate colds, sinusitis, and other upper respiratory infections.
